goal: Expose a single attribute-holder accessor to callers who need attribute information so external code can use the new class instead of many FileEntry methods, enabling future removal of simple delegating methods and improving cohesion.

ops.details: Extract an interface FileEntryAttributes in the same package that declares the attribute accessors: getLastModified(), getLastModifiedFileTime(), getLength(), getName(), isDirectory(), isExists(), refresh(File), setDirectory(boolean), setExists(boolean), setLastModified(FileTime), setLastModified(long), setLength(long), setName(String). Have FileEntryState implement FileEntryAttributes. This provides a stable, narrow type for callers to depend on.

Add a new accessor on FileEntry: public FileEntryAttributes getAttributes() { return state; } and update any internal uses to prefer state or getAttributes(). Do not remove existing public methods in this step; this provides a migration path for external clients to use the new attribute holder type.

Update related tests affected by this refactoring block so the configured build command passes. Adjust imports, package references, moved class references, constructors, and assertions only when required. Do not change production behavior through tests.
